title: Fish Tacos
subtitle: Baked seasoned tilapia with crunchy cabbage, avocado, and a tangy lime crema. Ready in under an hour.

ingredients.heading: Ingredients
ingredients.note: Makes 24 small tacos. Scale down by half for a weeknight dinner for 4.
items: 1 tsp sriracha, or more to taste
ingredients.callout: Tip: The lime crema recipe makes extra on purpose — it goes fast. Make it ahead and refrigerate until ready to serve.

body.html: Tilapia seasoned with cumin and cayenne, baked at 375°F until flaky. Served in toasted corn tortillas loaded with cabbage, avocado, tomato, and a creamy lime crema that ties it all together.

items: Make the lime crema while the fish cooks

body.html: Crowd-pleaser tip: Set up a topping bar so guests build their own tacos. Everything can be prepped while the fish bakes.

slides.title: Season the Fish
slides.checkboxLabel: Season fish, drizzle oil, dot with butter

items: Preheat oven to 375°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment.
Mix cumin, cayenne, salt, and pepper in a small bowl.
Lay fish on the baking sheet and season both sides with the spice mix.
Drizzle with olive oil and dot the top with butter.

body.html: Even seasoning: Sprinkle from a height for more uniform coverage — don't dump it all in one spot.

items: Bake at 375°F for 20–25 minutes until the fish flakes easily with a fork.
Optional: Broil for 3–5 minutes at the end for lightly browned edges.
Remove from oven and break fish into large chunks with a fork — don't over-shred.

body.html: Doneness: Fish is done when it flakes easily and is opaque all the way through. Thicker fillets may need the full 25 minutes.

items: Combine sour cream, mayo, lime juice, garlic powder, and sriracha in a bowl.
Taste and adjust sriracha for heat. Refrigerate until ready to use.

slides.title: Prep the Toppings
slides.checkboxLabel: Shred, slice, and dice all toppings

items: Cabbage: Shred thinly with a knife or mandoline.
Avocados: Halve, pit, and slice just before serving to avoid browning.
Cilantro: Remove thick stems, roughly chop or leave as leaves.
Heat a dry skillet over medium-high heat.
Toast tortillas one or two at a time, 30 seconds per side, until lightly charred and pliable.
Stack in a clean towel or tortilla warmer to keep warm.

body.html: Don't skip toasting: It adds flavor and makes tortillas pliable enough to fold without cracking.

slides.title: Assemble + Serve
slides.checkboxLabel: Build tacos, drizzle crema, squeeze lime

items: Place fish chunks in the center of each tortilla.
Top with cabbage, avocado, tomato, red onion, and cilantro.
Serve with a lime wedge for squeezing.

body.html: Serving tip: Lay out all toppings as a build-your-own bar — faster for crowds and everyone gets exactly what they want.
